source_channel.description: How student initially learned about institution

affordability_index.description: Financial attractiveness metric based on net cost (0-100 scale)

engagement_score.description: Digital engagement metric based on website/email interaction (0-100 scale)

completeness_score.description: Application completeness and quality indicator (0-100 scale)

offer_fit_score.description: Institutional fit prediction for offer (0-100 scale)

inquiry_to_app_prob.description: Simulated probability of applying given inquiry (0-1)

app_to_offer_prob.description: Simulated probability of receiving offer given application (0-1)

offer_to_enroll_prob.description: Simulated probability of enrolling given offer (0-1)

inquiry_flag.description: Student generated an inquiry (should be 1 for all records)

application_date.description: Date application submitted (YYYY-MM-DD), null if not applied

offer_date.description: Date offer sent (YYYY-MM-DD), null if no offer

enrollment_date.description: Date student enrolled (YYYY-MM-DD), null if did not enroll

days_inquiry_to_app.description: Days between inquiry and application submission

data_quality_notes.generation_method: Probabilistic simulation with realistic higher-ed funnel ranges
data_quality_notes.missing_values: Dates and day counts are null when a student did not progress to that stage

key_insights: Conversion rates vary significantly by source channel (Website, Search, Campus Visit, etc.)
Academic index and affordability index are strong drivers of enrollment
First-generation and financially-needy students show different progression patterns
Geographic origin impacts yield and conversion at each stage
Days-to-conversion metrics provide insights into engagement velocity
data_source: Synthetic dataset calibrated to real industry benchmarks (NACAC, EAB research)
